nameMeanings.name: Pope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English pope (derived via Old English from Late Latin papa ‘bishop, pope’, from Greek pappas ‘father’, in origin a nursery word.) In the early Christian Church, the Latin term was at first used as a title of respect for male clergy of every rank, but in the Western Church it gradually came to be restricted to bishops, and then only to the bishop of Rome; in the Eastern Church it continued to be used of all priests (see Popov , Papas ). The nickname would have been used for a vain or pompous man, or for someone who had played the part of the pope in a pageant or play. The surname is also present in Ireland and Scotland.

variants.name: Popov
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Nikolay, Oleg, Grigoriy, Anatoly, Andrei, Arkadi, Dmitriy, Mikhail, Sergey.  Bulgarian or Macedonian Atanas, Dimitar, Yordan.  Serbian Bosko, Bozidar, Branko.

Russian, Bulgarian, Macedonian, Serbian, and Rusyn: patronymic from pop ‘priest’, from Greek pappas ‘father’ (compare English Pope ). The name may sometimes derive from a nickname, but celibacy was not enjoined on priests of the Orthodox Church and so the name can indeed mean ‘son of the priest’. In many cases, however, the Russian name means no more than ‘of the priest’ and designates a servant of a priest. Compare Popoff , see also Pop 2, Popovic , and Popovski 1.
Jewish (American): most likely a shortened form of   Popovski   2. Compare   Popoff  .

variants.name: Papke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Otto, Willi.
North German (also Päpke): nickname from a diminutive of Low German pape, Dutch paepe ‘pope’. Compare Pope .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Pope was born in 1485, in Mildenhall, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry Pope, was 20 and his mother, Elizabeth Johnston, was 16. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Lady Alice Cornes Mardox. He died in 1570, in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 85.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert Pope est né en 1485, à Mildenhall, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Henry Pope, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Johnston, avait 16 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Lady Alice Cornes Mardox. Il est décédé en 1570, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 85 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert Pope 1485 in Mildenhall, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry Pope 20 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Johnston 16. Er hatte mit Lady Alice Cornes Mardox mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1570 im Alter von 85 Jahren in Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert Pope è nato il nel 1485 nel Mildenhall, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Henry Pope, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Johnston, aveva 16 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Lady Alice Cornes Mardox. Morì nel 1570, nel Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 85 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Pope(이)가 1485년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 미데홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Pope(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Johnston(은)는 16세였다. 그는 Lady Alice Cornes Mardox(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1570년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르에서 85세의 나이로 사망했다.
